title: Psychotria simianensis
intro: Psychotria simianensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the madagascar tea plant or monkey leaf. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Psychotria simianensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is typically used to make herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which are commonly consumed in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being.

The genus Psychotria is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world.
